meta.description: Identify and remove technical batch effects from multi-cohort expression matrices using ComBat, limma removeBatchEffect, or SVA. Inputs: expression matrix, batch labels. Outputs: corrected matrix, PCA/UMAP before-after plots, QC report.

scientific_integrity.detail: Scientific integrity check completed without reportable redline findings.

practice_boundaries.detail: Practice boundaries check completed without reportable redline findings.

methodological_ground.detail: Methodological ground check completed without reportable redline findings.

code_usability.detail: Bundled tests plus five live Rscript runs confirmed runnable, deterministic code paths.

functional_suitability.note: Post-ComBat normalization is appropriate here but could be justified more explicitly in the top-level skill contract.

reliability.note: Reliability was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

performance_context.note: Performance and context was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

agent_usability.note: Trigger intent is clear, but the skill lacks an explicit 'When to Use' heading.

human_usability.note: The workflow is intentionally strict on malformed study designs and does not try to auto-repair them.

security.note: No explicit privacy-handling note is provided for potentially sensitive sample metadata.

maintainability.note: Maintainability was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

agent_specific.note: Out-of-scope boundaries are strong, but invalid-design failures could include more explicit human handoff wording.

key_strengths: Strong scope control: the skill clearly defines supported merged-bulk use cases and excludes nearby unsafe scenarios such as single-cell integration.
Documentation and implementation stay aligned across CLI arguments, outputs, error codes, troubleshooting, and tests.
Reliability is high: the workflow validates design assumptions early, emits actionable SKILL_* failures, and records session information for reproducibility.
The QC package is practical and complete, giving users before/after boxplots, PCA, and clustering outputs rather than only a corrected matrix.

recommendations.title: Add an explicit When to Use section
recommendations.problem: Trigger guidance exists in the description, but some evaluators and orchestrators look specifically for a 'When to Use' section header.
recommendations.root_cause: Top-level structure emphasizes file-loading guidance more than explicit trigger guidance.
recommendations.fix: Add a short '## When to Use' block near the top and keep '## When to Read External Files' as the progressive-disclosure section.

recommendations.title: Document privacy handling for sample metadata
recommendations.problem: The workflow writes matched sample identifiers to CSV and PDF outputs without reminding users to de-identify sensitive clinical metadata when appropriate.
recommendations.root_cause: The skill is optimized for technical batch correction and currently omits explicit data-handling boundary guidance.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief warning in SKILL.md and references/troubleshooting.md advising de-identification before processing patient-linked metadata.

recommendations.title: Emit a machine-readable run summary
recommendations.problem: Automation currently has to infer success from logs and file existence checks.
recommendations.root_cause: The output contract is entirely file-based and human-readable.
recommendations.fix: Write a small run_summary.json or similar manifest with parameters, warnings, output paths, and final status.
